nl_statement: If f : E → ℝ is differentiable and ∇f is L-Lipschitz, then for all x, y ∈ E, f(y) ≤ f(x) + ⟨∇f(x), y-x⟩ + (L/2)‖y-x‖².
lean_signature: theorem descent_lemma_v2 {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E] {f : E → ℝ} {L : NNReal} (hf : Differentiable ℝ f) (hL : LipschitzWith L (fun z => gradient f z)) (x y : E) : f y ≤ f x + ⟪gradient f x, y - x⟫_ℝ + (L : ℝ) / 2 * ‖y - x‖^2
reuse_reason: core inequality used by every first-order optimization convergence proof
